sourceMovie.title: Nobody
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Nobody (2021) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Nobody clicked for you, John Wick (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Nobody clicked for you, John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mr. & Mrs. Smith
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Nobody clicked for you, Mr. & Mrs. Smith (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Nobody?
faq.answer: Try John Wick, John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum, Mr. & Mrs. Smith—each shares DNA with Nobody while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Nobody?
faq.answer: Nobody (2021) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Nobody part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Nobody Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
